name: Coconut Oats Dinner

name: Mutton Curry Recipe (Mutton Masala Gravy)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "½ kg (1.1 lbs.) mutton ((bone-in or boneless lamb/goat, cut to 1½ to 2 inch))", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons plain yogurt ((curd))", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "¾ tablespoon ginger garlic paste", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "¾ teaspoon salt ((use as needed))", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon turmeric", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon meat masala ((or garam masala))", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon Kashmiri red chili powder ((adjust to heat preference, leave out for low heat))", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "2 tablespoons oil", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "3 green cardamoms (/elaichi)", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "1 inch cinnamon", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "1 cup onions (fine chopped or processed )", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "¾ cup (1 large) tomatoes (pureed or chopped)", "source_position": 12}, {"raw_text": "1 to 2 green chilies (slit or chopped (optional, leave out for low heat))", "source_position": 13}, {"raw_text": "2 teaspoons meat masala (or garam masala ( I use Everest meat masala))", "source_position": 14}, {"raw_text": "2 sprigs curry leaves (or 1 bay leaf (optional))", "source_position": 15}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon salt (or as needed, to adjust later )", "source_position": 16}, {"raw_text": "1 to 2 tablespoons coriander leaves ( /cilantro chopped to garnish)", "source_position": 17}, {"raw_text": "¾ cup hot water (to cook in stovetop pressure cooker)", "source_position": 18}, {"raw_text": "2 to 2½ cups hot water (to cook in a regular pot/Dutch oven)", "source_position": 19}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Marinate mutton with salt, ginger garlic, turmeric, garam masala or meat masala, chili powder and curd. Yogurt & ginger garlic paste are natural meat tenderizers so they make the mutton succulent."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Set aside until you prepare the rest or leave it in the refrigerator to marinate overnight. (When you are ready to cook, leave the cold mutton out on the counter for 30 mins to bring the meat closer to room temperature.)"}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "On a low to medium flame, gently heat oil in a pan or pressure cooker. Add the whole spices - cinnamon, cardamoms and bay leaf (optional), followed by onions & green chilies."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Saute until onions turn golden. Lower the flame to very low as much as possible. Then transfer the marinated mutton and saute for 5 to 7 mins."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Stir in the meat masala & curry leaves (optional), followed by pureed tomatoes."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Increase the heat to medium and saute for another 5 to 7 mins, until the raw flavor of tomatoes has reduced. Pour hot water & mix well."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "To cook in a regular pot: Bring it to a gentle boil and cook covered on a medium heat for 1 hour 30 minutes. Stir well every 12 to 15 minutes and pour more hot water in batches as needed."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Stovetop Pressure Cooker: Pressure cook on a medium heat for 4 to 5 whistles, depending on the kind or cooker, meat and size of the mutton pieces."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "When the pressure drops, open the lid and mix well. If the curry is runny, cook/saute for a few more minutes to evaporate some of the moisture. Your mutton is cooked through when it is tender, flakes easily with a fork and falls off the bone easily."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Taste the curry and add more salt if needed. If you feel it is low on flavor, you may sprinkle little garam masala. Garnish with fine chopped coriander leaves."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Serve mutton curry hot with rice, tandoori roti, naan or plain paratha."}]

name: How to Make Curd (Dahi - Indian Yogurt)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "1 liter milk (full fat or whole milk)", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon curd /dahi (or yogurt as starter (increase to ¾ to 1 tbsp. if you are making this in winters))", "source_position": 2}]

This reduces the chances of milk solids getting stuck to the bottom."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Pour milk and bring it to boil on a medium to low flame."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "To get thick curd, once it comes to a boil simmer the milk for 15 mins on a very low flame."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Keep stirring in between else the milk will get burnt and smell bad."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Simmering step is to get a very thick curd. You can skip this if you are ok to have a moderately thick yogurt."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Allow the milk to cool down."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "If you are intending to use a curd culture that has come down to room temperature, then allow the boiled milk to come down to luke warm temp."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "But if you are using the culture directly from the fridge, then the temperature of the milk must be mildly warmer than the luke warm."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Immerse your clean finger in the milk and check. or Your food thermometer should ideally read 110 to 115 F (or 43 to 46 C). Immerse your thermometer into the milk and check."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Add a tsp of curd to the warm milk and stir well. cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Add 2 cups chilled curd to a deep bowl or a blender. Do note that using sour curd may need addition of milk and more sugar. I used a whisk here. You can also use a immersion blender or mixing rod."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Next add 3 to 4 tablespoons sugar, 1 pinch of saffron strands or 1 teaspoon rosewater. If you do not have saffron strands or rose water, simply skip both."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add ¼ to ½ teaspoon cardamom powder. I removed the husks of the 10 whole green cardamoms & powdered them with little sugar in a grinder. Taste test and add more sugar or cardamom powder to suit your taste. Or simply blend in a blender until frothy. You can garnish with any of these – saffron, chopped nuts or cardamom powder."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "On a low heat, dry roast jeera/ cumin seeds until they begin to smell good and aromatic. cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Grease or rinse a heavy bottom pot and pour milk to it."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Bring it to a boil then reduce the flame to low. Add almonds and pistachios."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Turn off the heat and rest the nuts until the skin loosens (for about 10 to 15 mins). Drain them and peel the skin. (check the step-by-step pictures above or video)"}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Next stir once to prevent the milk from getting scorched at the bottom."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Continue to simmer the milk, slide the layer of cream to the sides of the pot every time it forms and repeat sticking it up to the side. name: Chapati Recipe (Indian Flatbread)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "2 cups chakki atta ((stone ground whole wheat flour))", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "¾ cup warm water ((or hot water, 2 to 4 tbsps more as required read notes))", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "¼ teaspoon salt ((optional))", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "1 to 1½ tablespoons oil ((optional, read notes))", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "2 to 2½ tablespoons ghee (or oil)", "source_position": 5}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Add atta to a wide mixing bowl & make a well in the center. Add oil and salt if using. Pour warm or hot water and begin to mix. If using hot water use a spoon first and then your hand."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Mix well to form a soft dough, splashing more water only as required. Knead the dough for a minute or 2. It has to be soft and not sticky or crumbly."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Wet your hands and smear water all over the dough ball. Cover and rest for a minimum of 30 mins. You can leave up to 2 hours at room temperature."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "When you are ready to make the chapati, knead the dough until soft and pliable. If the dough looks dry, moisten your palm and knead. It should not be sticky."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Divide the dough to 6 portions to make layered chapati or to 8 portions to make single layer chapati. Gently roll the balls in between your palms to smoothen them. Cover until you use up."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Slightly flatten a ball and dip in flour to coat it with flour all over. Shake it gently to dust off the excess flour."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Begin to roll the dough evenly without putting pressure. After 2 to 3 rolls, lift it and move it in clock-wise direction a little. Continue to roll it and then move it in clock-wise direction. If the dough begins to stick to the rolling pin or to the kitchen counter, sprinkle little flour. (not too much)"}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Single Layer Chapati: Repeat these steps until you make a 7 to 8 inch chapati."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Layered Chapati: After rolling the dough to 4 to 5 inches disc, smear melted ghee or a few drops of oil and then sprinkle 2 pinches of flour. Fold it to half, to make a half circle. Smear oil and flour and fold again to make a triangle. (Check the pictures). Sprinkle a bit of flour and roll to a 7 inch chapati."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "After rolling the chapati, take it to one of your hands & dust the excess flour with the other hand. Transfer to a dry place, away from wind and heat. After rolling 3 to 4 begin to cook."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Heat a tawa/ pan on a medium high heat. It should be hot enough but not smoking."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Gently transfer the chapati to the hot tawa and wait until you see a few bubbles. Using a spatula, turn it to the other side. Spread little oil or ghee all over the chapati. Let cook for a minute or 2. (If required regulate the flame to medium. This depends on the kind of stove & pan.)"}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Turn back to the other side. It will ideally begin to puff. Press down with a spatula gently in place where it doesn't puff up. This helps the chapati to cook and puff. Keep rotating it and cook on the edges too."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Spread some ghee or oil all over and turn it to the other side. Spread ghee here as well. You will begin to see some smoke from the pan. It is normal. Remove to a plate."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Before cooking the next chapati, reduce the heat and wipe your tawa with a thick clean cloth or tissues. Be careful while you do this. To avoid this step, make sure you dust off the excess flour from the chapati after rolling."}, {"step_number": 16, "instruction": "Stack the hot chapatis on a plate. Once done cooking all of them, fold each to a triangle, while still hot. Transfer to a container & place over a cooling rack. Cover partially so they don't sweat. This keep them soft."}, {"step_number": 17, "instruction": "Serve chapati with curries, stew, dal or use them to make wraps, rolls etc."}]

Give a good stir and reduce the flame."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Soon the froth evaporates and butter begins to bubble rapidly."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Within few minutes, the color of the butter begins to change from yellow to light golden."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "At this stage, the milk solids begin to settle at the bottom & the bubbles will reduce significantly. You can see them in the picture. I have shown little milk solids in the ladle. When they just begin to turn light brown, remove the pot from the stove."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Ghee will continue to cook in the hot pot and brown the milk solids. Let the temperature cool down a bit."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Place a clean and dry strainer/filter over a clean jar. Pour the ghee through the strainer/ filter. Press down the milk solids. I usually strain the ghee to several small jars. This helps the ghee to remain fresh for longer."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Culture the cream : Follow either method (A) or (B) or (C) based on your convenience. Methods B and C are traditionally followed."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Method A – Heat up 2 cups of heavy cream until warm enough. Stir in 2 tablespoons of yogurt (at room temperature). Cover and keep it in a warm place for 8 to 16 hours as you would keep to make yogurt. When your cultured cream is ready, it smells like yogurt. Chill it for few hours and then proceed to step 2 to make your own cultured butter."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Method B – Boil non-homogenized full fat milk. When the temperature comes down you will see a layer of cream over it. Make yogurt using both the cream and milk, following this post on homemade yogurt or curd ."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "When the yogurt is set remove the top portion of the cultured cream and store in a separate jar. Refrigerate and keep adding the cream to it every time you make yogurt. You will need at least a cup of this cultured cream to go ahead and make your butter, followed by ghee."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Method C – Boil milk and let it cool down for a few hours. Remove the milk cream that forms on top & store in a separate jar. Refrigerate this and keep adding more cream every time you boil the milk."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "When you have a cup of this cream, heat it to warm temperature and then add a tbsp of yogurt/curd culture to this and mix well. Allow this to set in a warm place like regular yogurt for 5 to 8 hours depending on the temperature."}]

name: How to make Paneer
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "2 liters (8 cups) full fat milk", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "2 to 3 tablespoons vinegar ((or lemon juice or ½ cup yogurt or 4 to 5 grams citric acid diluted in 1 cup water))", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "1 colander ((strainer))", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "1 cheese cloth ((muslin cloth or fresh handkerchief))", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "1 large bowl ((to collect whey))", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "1 to 2 heavy objects", "source_position": 6}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Heat Milk: Pour milk to a heavy bottom pot & bring it to a gentle boil on a medium heat. Stir occasionally to ensure the milk doesn't get scorched at the bottom."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Curdle Milk: When the milk comes to a boil, turn off the stove and stir in 2 tablespoons of vinegar. You should see the milk begins to split, separating the solids and the whey. If done correctly, whey should be clear or yellowish but not milky."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Troubleshooting: If the milk did not curdle fully or still looks milky, stir 1 tbsp more vinegar. Boil on a medium heat till it curdles fully. Turn off immediately and do not overcook else paneer can become hard."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Strain & Rinse: Place a colander over a large bowl to collect the whey. Spread a cheese cloth over it. Transfer the paneer and the whey in 2 batches. Pour cold water all over the paneer to rinse off the vinegar really well."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Drain the Liquid: Gather the edges of the cloth in your hand and bundle the paneer to form a round shape. Squeeze off any excess liquid. Make a knot and hang the paneer on to your faucet, for 30 mins to drain completely. (see post for pictures)"}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Set the Paneer: Remove the knot and twist the edges of the cloth. Place bundle on a flat colander or a wooden board. Press down the cloth & place a heavy object (like a cast iron pot & rice bags) on it. (check the video below) Let the paneer set at room temperature or in the refrigerator (if hot) for 3 to 4 hours,"}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Dice the Paneer: Unpack the cheese from the cloth. You will see a dent and it is normal. Cut the paneer to 1 inch cubes. Refrigerate in a air tight container and use within 2 to 3 weeks or freeze up to 3 months."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Recipes to try: Use the diced cheese in curries, kebabs or rice pilafs. You may check out these 60 amazing Paneer Recipes for ideas."}]

name: Turmeric Tea Recipe (Golden Tea with No-Brew Option )
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "¼ to ½ teaspoon turmeric powder ((or 1 inch fresh turmeric root chopped/sliced))", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "½ teaspoon (½ inch root) fresh ginger ((chopped or ¼ teaspoon dried ginger for no brew option))", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "⅛ teaspoon ground black pepper ((3 to 4 whole pepper corn crushed))", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "½ to 1 teaspoon ghee (or cold pressed extra virgin oil (avocado/ olive/coconut))", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "1¼ cup water", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon lemon juice (or ¼ cup warm/slightly hot orange juice or as needed (optional))", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "1 teaspoon honey or jaggery to taste ((optional))", "source_position": 7}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "You can make turmeric tea either with ground turmeric or with fresh turmeric rhizome, which you will peel to cut down the bitter taste. Slice or chop 1 inch root or use ¼ to ½ teaspoon ground turmeric/ powder. Crush or crack 3 to 4 black pepper corn. Choose your fats – ghee or cold pressed oil like extra virgin coconut oil, olive oil or avocado, depending on your diet. Pepper and ghee help in better absorption. I prefer to use ghee for flavor and fats prevent dehydration."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Slice or chop half inch fresh ginger (or use ¼ teaspoon dried ground ginger). I am using lemon juice here but you can also use ¼ orange juice."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Mix together 1¼ cup of water, turmeric, black pepper, ginger and fats. If you want you can also add the ghee/oil later just before straining the tea."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Mix well."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Optionally If you want you can use crushed spices like – 1 cardamom, 1 clove, 1 inch True cinnamon (not cassia), ¼ teaspoon each of cumin, carom seeds and fennel seeds. All these spices work on the digestive and respiratory system. They also help to detox the body. You need to coarsely crush the spices using a mortar pestle before adding to the pot."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Bring the mixture to a boil and reduce the heat to medium. Ground turmeric requires only 4 mins, and chopped turmeric requires about 8 mins of boiling. If using spices, you need to boil for 10 mins."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Remove from the heat and strain the turmeric tea."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Press down the ingredients to extract all of the turmeric goodness. There are plenty of add-ins that both enhance the taste and the nutritional content of the tea."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "You may add jaggery or palm jaggery while boiling. name: Indian Butter Chickpeas

cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "You will need the following ingredients:"}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "2 – 15 oz cans chickpeas (drained or 3 cups boiled chickpeas from 1 cup dried)"}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "1½ to 2 teaspoon Kashmiri chili powder (adjust to heat tolerance, I use 2 tsps home ground)"}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Omit this step if you are using nut butter or heavy cream as a cashew substitute. (While they soak, proceed with the next step.)"}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Begin to melt 2 tablespoons butter on a low flame. Before it melts completely, add ginger and garlic. Sauté for 1 to 2 mins without burning."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Stir in the ground spices – red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder and salt."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Add the tomato paste, baking soda (optional), 1 cup water (or chickpea cooked liquid) & chickpeas. (Or Add 1¼ cup passata/tomato puree and omit water)"}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Mix well and bring to a gentle boil on a medium heat."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Cover and cook for 10 mins, until your sauce is really thick and the chickpeas are tender. (While this cooks, grind the soaked cashews.)"}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Stir in the cashew puree/nut butter and heavy cream (start with lower amount of cream and adjust as per taste & consistency). cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Pluck 1 cup mint leaves (tightly packed, 15 grams). We don’t want to use stalks here. Add them to a bowl of water and rinse them very well a few times. Rinse half cup coriander leaves with tender stalks (tightly packed, 15 grams), a few times. Drain completely and add them to a grinder jar."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Add ¼ inch ginger, 1 small garlic clove and 1 to 2 green chilies."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Add ½ teaspoon sugar and 1/3 teaspoon salt."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add 1 tablespoon chilled thick yogurt. Do not use runny yogurt. If you double or triple the recipe then add yogurt accordingly. Do not add too much yogurt at this stage."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Grind until smooth. Do not over blend. Over blending may cause the mint leaves to release oils and can taste bitter. Keep this aside. If you want you can serve this as is or even refrigerate at this stage for later."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Add the rest of the thick yogurt (1 cup minus 1 tbsp) to a bowl. Add ½ to ¾ teaspoon roasted cumin and ½ to ¾ teaspoon chaat masala. Skip chaat masala if you don’t have. If you want you can reduce the quantity of yogurt but this is what we like at home."}, {"step_number": 7, "instruction": "Whisk very well until smooth."}, {"step_number": 8, "instruction": "Add the mint chutney to the yogurt."}, {"step_number": 9, "instruction": "Mix well and taste test. Adjust salt and spice to taste. Serve mint chutney as a side."}, {"step_number": 10, "instruction": "Wash pudina in a pot filled with clean water. Repeat the wash a few times and drain them in a colander."}, {"step_number": 11, "instruction": "Dry roast ¾ tbsp each of chana dal and urad dal on a low flame until golden. Add ½ tsp cumin to the hot pan and switch off. Cool these. If using bhuna chana skip roasting. You can also saute pudina in oil for 2 to 3 mins first and use. You can also use peanuts. Just roast the peanuts until aromatic and golden."}, {"step_number": 12, "instruction": "Add ½ cup chopped coconut, 2 to 3 green chilies, ½ inch ginger or 2 small garlic cloves, pudina, roasted dal, cumin and salt. You can also add a tsp of tamarind paste if you like the tang."}, {"step_number": 13, "instruction": "Blend them all together first for pudina chutney."}, {"step_number": 14, "instruction": "Add water as needed and blend to a smooth or coarse chatni to suit your liking. I prefer to use lemon juice now. Transfer the pudina chutney to a serving bowl."}, {"step_number": 15, "instruction": "Pudina chutney can be served without tempering too. To temper heat a small pan with little oil on a low flame. Add red chili, ¼ tsp mustard and ½ tsp urad dal. When the dal turns golden, add hing and switch off the stove. Pour this to the chutney. Mix well. Serve pudina chutney with south Indian breakfasts like idli dosa evening snacks"}]

name: Cumin Powder (Roasted Jeera Powder)
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "½ cup cumin (seeds (72 grams))", "source_position": 1}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Clean cumin seeds and add them to a heavy bottom pan. Turn on the heat to medium and keep stirring until the seeds become hot to touch."}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Reduce the heat to the lowest and roast them. Keep stirring continuously so the seeds roast evenly."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "When done, they will emit a strong earthy, pungent and smoky aroma. Remove from heat. Do not over roast they will burn and taste bitter."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Cool them completely and add to a grinder. Grind in short intervals of 30 to 50 seconds until ground to a fine powder."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Cool completely and transfer to a air tight glass jar. If you plan to store for longer, store in smaller jars and use them one after the other so most of the aroma is retained."}, {"step_number": 6, "instruction": "Use cumin powder as needed in curries, stir fry dishes, soups, salads and chaats."}]

name: Dahi Kabab | Dahi Ke Kabab
ingredients_json: [{"raw_text": "2 cups curd ((plain yogurt or ¾ cup Greek yogurt or hung curd))", "source_position": 1}, {"raw_text": "1¼ cup paneer (crumbled (or ¾ cup bread crumbs))", "source_position": 2}, {"raw_text": "10 cashewnuts (chopped finely)", "source_position": 3}, {"raw_text": "¼ cup onions ((optional) very finely chopped)", "source_position": 4}, {"raw_text": "1½ teaspoons ginger paste", "source_position": 5}, {"raw_text": "2 green chilies (chopped or paste (use as needed))", "source_position": 6}, {"raw_text": "1 to 1 ¼ teaspoons garam masala", "source_position": 7}, {"raw_text": "3 tablespoons coriander leaves (– finely chopped)", "source_position": 8}, {"raw_text": "Salt as needed", "source_position": 9}, {"raw_text": "½ cup bread crumbs ((or substitute with powdered poha))", "source_position": 10}, {"raw_text": "2 to 3 tablespoons besan ((gram flour) (or substitute with sattu, roasted chana dal flour))", "source_position": 11}, {"raw_text": "Oil as needed for shallow frying", "source_position": 12}]
cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Place a strainer over a large deep bowl. Spread a thin muslin cloth or cheese cloth over the strainer. Pour the curd and bring together the edges of the cloth. Then wring the cloth to drain up excess whey from curd. (check pics in the post)"}, {"step_number": 2, "instruction": "Place 2 to 3 heavy ceramic bowl or a heavy object over the pack of yogurt. Refrigerate the whole thing for 12 to 14 hours. The whey drips and gets collected to the bowl. Later hung curd is ready."}, {"step_number": 3, "instruction": "Crumble paneer and add it to a mixing bowl along with hung curd."}, {"step_number": 4, "instruction": "Add all the other ingredients - ginger, garlic, chilli, garam masala, onions, cashews and coriander leaves."}, {"step_number": 5, "instruction": "Mix well just to make a non sticky dough. name: Hara Bhara Kabab Recipe (Veg Kabab)

cook_steps: [{"step_number": 1, "instruction": "Pluck and rinse the spinach well in lots of water thrice. Transfer them to a colander to ensure all the water has drained.
